title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Failure Case: Source Authority
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt source authority increment in a repository context map and retrieval plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task.
Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Source Authority evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-source-authority-1uvtfe" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Source Authority into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-source-authority-1uvtfe
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
